recipe.title: Inside-Out Crispy Rice Paper Rolls

ingredients.name: rice paper wrappers
ingredients.note: standard round wrappers assumed; softened briefly, not soaked floppy

ingredients.name: cooked jasmine rice, chilled
ingredients.note: spread thinly on lower half of wrapper for inside-out crisp texture

ingredients.name: tamari
ingredients.note: 2 tablespoons for tofu, 1 tablespoon for sauce

ingredients.name: maple syrup
ingredients.note: 1 teaspoon in tofu, 1 teaspoon in sauce

steps.text: Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat and fill a shallow bowl with warm water.
steps.visualCue: clean island setup, warm water bowl, black induction surface ready
steps.textureCue: quiet anticipation before glossy rice paper and skillet sizzle
steps.runwayRisk: Too much setup can feel like neutral mise en place; compress into context only.

steps.text: Season crumbled tofu with tamari, sesame oil, ginger, garlic, and maple syrup; combine cabbage, carrot, cucumber, green onion, cilantro, and mint separately.
steps.visualCue: tan tofu crumbles becoming glossy, purple cabbage and orange carrot ribbons falling together
steps.textureCue: savory moist tofu against dry crisp vegetable ribbons
steps.runwayRisk: Watery vegetables could read soggy; prompts must show dry, springy ribbons.

steps.text: Dip one rice paper wrapper briefly until pliable and lay it on the work surface.
steps.visualCue: translucent wrapper softening from brittle disc into satin sheet
steps.textureCue: just-flexible rice paper, glossy but not floppy
steps.runwayRisk: Over-soaked wrapper may tear or puddle; lock short dip and intact round sheet.

steps.text: Spread about 2 tablespoons chilled rice over the lower half, add tofu and vegetable-herb filling, fold sides, and roll tightly.
steps.visualCue: thin rice layer clinging near the outside, tight strip of tofu and rainbow vegetables inside
steps.textureCue: sticky chilled grains, crisp fresh filling, taut translucent wrapper
steps.runwayRisk: Inside-out geometry may drift; repeat rice-on-lower-half and tight 10-12 cm roll scale.

steps.text: Pan-fry rolls seam-side down in light oil for 2 to 3 minutes per side until golden, crisp, and lightly blistered.
steps.visualCue: seam kisses hot pan, tiny bubbles, golden freckles, tongs turning each roll
steps.textureCue: crackly blistered rice paper, chewy toasted rice spots, light oil sheen
steps.runwayRisk: Model may invent deep-frying or flames; lock shallow skillet, no flame, no oil bath.

steps.text: Whisk peanut butter, tamari, lime juice, maple syrup, warm water, and optional red pepper flakes until smooth and creamy.
steps.visualCue: peanut paste loosens into glossy tan sauce ribbon with lime shine
steps.textureCue: silky spoon-coating sauce, thick but dip-ready
steps.runwayRisk: Sauce can look grainy or watery; lock smooth creamy viscosity.

steps.text: Rest rolls briefly, serve warm with peanut lime sauce, and optionally slice diagonally to reveal colorful layers.
steps.visualCue: diagonal cut face shows purple cabbage, carrot, cucumber, tofu, herbs, rice-speckled crisp exterior
steps.textureCue: crisp shell crack, cool juicy crunch, glossy peanut sauce coat
steps.runwayRisk: Final roll scale and filling colors can drift; lock 12 small hand-rolls, 10-12 cm long, not burrito-sized.

assumptions: Use standard round rice paper wrappers; final cooked rolls are treated as small hand rolls about 10-12 cm long and 3-4 cm diameter, not burrito-sized and not miniature spring-roll sticks.

The creator's 'really sexy and food porn' direction is interpreted as sensual macro lighting, glossy sauce, crackly texture, rich ASMR, and appetite-driven pacing without explicit or body-focused imagery.

No recipe-specific generated intermediate reference images are planned at ingest because the dish geometry is manageable with explicit prompt scale locks and existing kitchen, character, pose, and utensil assets.
Optional red pepper flakes stay visually subtle; they should not turn the peanut-lime sauce into a red chili sauce.
criticalTransformations: Rice paper changes from dry brittle round sheet to just-pliable glossy wrapper after a very brief warm-water dip.
Chilled jasmine rice is spread thinly over the lower half of each wrapper so grains sit near the exterior and toast into tiny crisp-chewy spots.
Seasoned tofu crumbles become glossy and savory while staying crumbly, not mashed into paste.
Dry vegetable and herb ribbons remain crisp and colorful inside the roll instead of steaming wet or collapsing.
Tight rolling creates a small sealed cylinder about 10-12 cm long and 3-4 cm diameter, not oversized and not miniature.
Light pan-frying in a nonstick skillet turns rice paper golden, blistered, and crackly without deep-frying or flame.
Peanut butter, lime, tamari, maple, and warm water emulsify into a smooth spoon-coating sauce that ribbons slowly.
visualTextureOpportunities: Opening crack: a golden blistered roll snaps as it breaks the surface of glossy peanut-lime sauce.
Translucent rice paper goes satin-soft over the light terrazzo island in an overhead macro.
Cold rice grains press into the wrapper like tiny pearls before becoming toasted freckles in the pan.
Purple cabbage, orange carrot, pale cucumber, green herbs, and tan tofu create a high-color cut-face reveal.
First seam-down skillet contact gives a sharp sizzle and immediate steam sparkle.
Tongs turn a roll to expose blistered golden patches and taut crackly skin.
Peanut-lime sauce loosens from thick paste to a glossy tan ribbon around the whisk.
Final diagonal half drags through sauce, leaving a thick shiny coat on the crisp shell.
possibleHooks: Macro crack-and-dip of a blistered roll entering peanut-lime sauce, then hard cut to the inside-out rice layer that explains the trick.
Tongs flip a roll to reveal sudden golden blisters and rice freckles forming on the outside.
Diagonal cut-face reveal shows rainbow vegetables inside a crisp rice-paper shell with sauce dripping slowly.
Satin rice paper sheet receives a thin layer of chilled rice, creating the inside-out setup before the sexy skillet payoff.
promptPolicySources: recipe-ingest: reconstruct sub-recipes, visible actions, scale assumptions, and clarifying questions from the URL source.
seedance-workflow: use Seedance 2 References mode, 9:16 1080p, 5-10 segments, hard cuts, timing, ASMR audio only, and explicit reference roles.
asset-reference-system: use existing kitchen, character, pose, expression, and utensil assets; each segment includes KitchenLayoutContextWide plus a shot-specific kitchen view.
food-physics-reference: specify crispness, gloss, moisture, sauce viscosity, blistering, and roll scale locks with negative constraints.
tiktok-food-direction: texture-first hook, 75-80 percent detail shots, context shots only when they prepare detail payoffs, and texture payoff every 3-5 scenes.
suno-music-generation: produce manual copy-ready Suno JSON and Markdown prompts only, no API calls.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the final rolls follow standard 22 cm rice-paper wrapper scale, yielding cooked rolls about 10-12 cm long and 3-4 cm diameter, or do you want a smaller party-bite format?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Exact roll scale affects Seedance scale locks, cutting reveal composition, and plating count.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the optional red pepper flakes be visible in the peanut-lime sauce, or should the sauce stay smooth tan and mild-looking?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Visible chili changes color palette and the final sauce prompt.
